<thought>
I am facing north at row 7, column D. Directly ahead (row 6, column D) is lava, so moving forward is unsafe. To my left is a wall (column C all '#'). To my right is floor (row 7, column E). Turning right to face east will allow me to move onto safe ground and explore further, possibly finding a path around the lava and toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is not visible in the current observation. My current position is at row 07, column D, facing up. Directly ahead, the cell at row 06, column D is a safe floor (`.`). The cells to my left (column C) and further left (column A) contain lava, and the cell to my right (column E) is a wall (`#`). Since the forward path is clear and safe, moving forward is the best option to explore and potentially locate the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

</thought>
<action>
